stem: How many principles of ethical behavior does this chapter list and describe?
explanation: 教材列出并描述七大道德原则:诚信、客观、胜任、公平、保密、专业、勤勉。
sourceRef: RES5 Study Text Part II Ch.16 — Ethical Behavior

stem: The word 'integrity' is said to derive from which mathematical concept?
explanation: 教材 2.2 指出 "integrity" 源自数学整数(integer)概念,意为完整、整全、未受减损的状态。
sourceRef: RES5 Study Text Part II Ch.16 — Principle of Integrity

stem: What are the two main obstacles to objectivity for a financial adviser representative?

options.A: Cognitive bias and conflict of interest
options.B: Lack of training and lack of time

explanation: 教材 3.4 明确客观性的两大障碍是(a)认知偏差与(b)利益冲突。
sourceRef: RES5 Study Text Part II Ch.16 — Principle of Objectivity

stem: Which is described as one of the most common cognitive biases, where a person seeks and interprets information that supports an assumption he already holds?
explanation: 教材 3.7 点名 "confirmation bias"(确认偏差)为最常见的认知偏差之一:只寻找并解读支持既有假设的信息。其余选项虽是真实偏差,但非教材所述。
sourceRef: RES5 Study Text Part II Ch.16 — Principle of Objectivity (Cognitive Bias)

stem: Under the principle of competence, what should a financial adviser representative do when asked to advise on a product outside his area of expertise?

options.A: Proceed, since the client has already asked
options.B: Refuse to act outside his knowledge and expertise and, if needed, upgrade his competency first
options.C: Give the advice but disclaim all responsibility
options.D: Charge a higher fee to compensate for the risk

explanation: 教材 4.4–4.5 要求顾问只在胜任范围内行事,有道德勇气坦承所不懂并拒绝越界;Andy 例(未受训即做遗产规划)说明应先提升能力。
sourceRef: RES5 Study Text Part II Ch.16 — Principle of Competence

stem: The principle of fairness revolves around three concepts. Which set correctly lists them?

options.B: Equality, the Golden Rule, and giving a person what is due to him

explanation: 教材 5.1 指出公平围绕三概念:平等、黄金法则、给予每个人应得之物。
sourceRef: RES5 Study Text Part II Ch.16 — Principle of Fairness

stem: Under the concept of 'equality' within the principle of fairness, charging one client higher fees than another is acceptable when:

options.A: The client belongs to a different race or religion
options.B: The difference is justified by good reasons, such as more complex needs requiring additional resources
options.C: The adviser simply prefers that client less
options.D: Any difference is acceptable without explanation

explanation: 教材 5.2–5.3 指出平等要求"相似之事相似处理",差别对待须有正当理由(如需求复杂、需额外资源);按种族或宗教差别收费则引发道德问题。
sourceRef: RES5 Study Text Part II Ch.16 — Principle of Fairness (Equality)

stem: Under the PDPA, an INDIVIDUAL found guilty of an offence in breach of its provisions is liable to:

options.A: A fine of up to S$5,000 and/or imprisonment of up to two years
options.B: A fine of up to S$50,000 only
options.C: A fine of S$1 million, or 10% of annual turnover, whichever is greater
options.D: A fine of up to S$25,000 and/or imprisonment of up to 12 months

explanation: 教材 6.7 规定个人违反 PDPA 可处最高 S$5,000 罚款及/或最高两年监禁。选项 C 是公司的罚则(S$100 万或年营业额 10%,取较高者)。
sourceRef: RES5 Study Text Part II Ch.16 — Principle of Confidentiality (PDPA)

stem: Under the PDPA, a COMPANY found in breach of its provisions is, upon conviction, liable to a fine of:

options.C: S$1 million, or 10% of its gross annual turnover in Singapore, whichever is greater
options.D: S$1 million, or 10% of its gross annual turnover in Singapore, whichever is lower

explanation: 教材 6.7 规定公司违反 PDPA 经定罪可处 S$1,000,000 罚款,或其在新加坡年总营业额的 10%,以较高者为准。选项 D 错在"以较低者"。
sourceRef: RES5 Study Text Part II Ch.16 — Principle of Confidentiality (PDPA)

stem: The principle of professionalism has two requirements. One is to bring trust and dignity to the profession; the other is to:

options.A: Maximise commission income from each client
options.B: Treat all persons with respect and consideration, not as 'mere means' to an end
options.C: Always agree with whatever the client proposes
options.D: Keep all client information secret even from regulators

explanation: 教材 7.2–7.4 指出专业原则两要求:以尊重对待所有人、不把人当作"纯粹手段";以及以带来信任与尊严的方式行事。
sourceRef: RES5 Study Text Part II Ch.16 — Principle of Professionalism

stem: Which of the following is NOT one of the three components of the principle of diligence?

options.A: Providing services in a reasonably prompt and thorough manner
options.C: Ensuring support staff conduct themselves professionally
options.D: Guaranteeing a minimum investment return to every client

explanation: 教材 8.1–8.9 列出勤勉三要素:及时周全提供服务、以应有审慎(due care)行事、确保支持人员专业。保证最低投资回报并非其一,且属误导/虚假承诺。
sourceRef: RES5 Study Text Part II Ch.16 — Principle of Diligence

stem: What practical guidance does the principle of diligence give regarding the expectations a representative sets about support services?

options.A: Always promise the highest possible level of service to win the sale
options.B: Do not over-promise and under-deliver; set reasonable and achievable expectations
options.C: Leave all support work to the back office without any oversight
options.D: Avoid setting any expectations to remain flexible

explanation: 教材 8.9–8.10 强调设定切合实际、可达成的期望,"不要过度承诺、交付不足";过度承诺不专业,且可能构成误导/虚假陈述招致民刑责任。
sourceRef: RES5 Study Text Part II Ch.16 — Principle of Diligence

stem: According to the chapter, why is trust especially important in the financial services industry?

options.A: Because regulators require a written trust agreement for every sale
options.B: Because products are intangible and clients cannot easily assess the suitability of advice or products themselves
options.C: Because all financial products are guaranteed by the government
options.D: Because clients always understand financial products better than advisers

explanation: 教材 9.3–9.4 指出金融产品无形,客户难以自行确信地评估建议或产品的适当性,故信任尤为重要;若客户能自行评估,便无需建议。
sourceRef: RES5 Study Text Part II Ch.16 — Benefits of Ethical Behavior

stem: Which of the following is identified in the chapter as a benefit of ethical behavior in business?

options.A: Higher commission rates mandated by regulators
options.C: Building client loyalty, enhancing reputation, retaining good employees and avoiding legal problems
options.D: The right to keep client funds without accountability

explanation: 教材 9.5 列出道德行为的益处:建立客户忠诚、提升声誉、留住好员工、营造正向工作环境与避免法律问题。其余选项均非教材所述,且与道德相悖。
sourceRef: RES5 Study Text Part II Ch.16 — Benefits of Ethical Behavior
